title: Item Stitching Fix
description: Removes the annoying gap in Item Models!
body:
Item Stitching Fix

As everyone knows, items are of course a thing in Minecraft, their model usually consisting of a 2D texture that the game automatically stretches out to be a single pixel wide. This is great, and works really well, but it is quite buggy. One of these main bugs is called item stitching- the top of the model being a bit off from the sides, creating an ugly transparent gap that's subtle but definitely there enough to drive you insane. Many people are reasonably annoyed by this stitching, myself included, so I decided to create a mod that fixes it- the very mod you're looking at now! This mod slaps the faces of the models back into place, preventing all ugly texture stitching and subtly making your game much more playable. *~ Description by EpicNecromancer*

This mod is fully customizable, allowing you to select the algorithm with which you would like to fix this glitch! As seen above, this bug has been around since as early as Minecraft 1.8!

More Info

This mod is a port of a fabric mod, Item Model Fix by PepperCode1!
To find out more and talk to an awesome community, join the Discord!
